addedPages.label: Support Page
addedPages.via: Left Side bar: Bottom Right corner (?) > Click on Support

login-draft-0.expected: The Devin logo, Welcome heading, tenant description, work email field, and Continue button are visible and readable.

login-draft-1.steps: Inspect the available login methods on the Login page.
login-draft-1.expected: The work email field and at least the GitHub and Google login buttons are visible with the correct icons and labels.

login-draft-2.steps: Inspect the account registration prompt on the Login page.
login-draft-2.expected: "Don't have an account? Sign up" is visible and readable.

login-draft-3.steps: Click the Sign up link on the Login page.
login-draft-3.expected: The URL contains /signup/, and "Already have an account? Log in" is visible and readable.

login-draft-4.steps: Submit each invalid email value separately: abc, a@, and spaces only.
login-draft-4.expected: Each invalid email is rejected with clear validation, and the user remains on the Login page.

login-draft-5.steps: Load the Login page and inspect the URL, visible UI, and browser console before and after submitting an invalid email.
login-draft-5.expected: No credentials, OTPs, sensitive tokens, or internal error details appear in the URL, UI, or browser console.

user-support-page-mrkjukp2-draft-0.steps: From the Landing search page, open the left sidebar and click the (?) help control in the bottom-right corner. 2. Click Support to open /org/cog-enterprise-qa/settings/support. 3. Click the support-chat launcher on the Support page.
user-support-page-mrkjukp2-draft-0.expected: Support chat widget opens and is ready to accept a message; no console errors.

user-support-page-mrkjukp2-draft-1.steps: Open the Support page (left sidebar → bottom-right (?) help control → Support). 2. Locate the Documentation card/heading on the page.
user-support-page-mrkjukp2-draft-1.expected: The "Documentation" card heading is rendered and visible on the Support page.

user-support-page-mrkjukp2-draft-2.steps: Open the Support page (left sidebar → bottom-right (?) help control → Support). 2. Read the description text under the Documentation card.
user-support-page-mrkjukp2-draft-2.expected: The text "Find answers to common questions and comprehensive guides" is visible under the Documentation card.

user-support-page-mrkjukp2-draft-3.steps: Open the Support page (left sidebar → bottom-right (?) help control → Support). 2. Confirm the Documentation button is visible and enabled. 3. Click the Documentation button.
user-support-page-mrkjukp2-draft-3.expected: The Documentation button is clickable and navigates to the Documentation page; the target route loads without error.

user-support-page-mrkjukp2-draft-4.steps: Reload the Support page (left sidebar → bottom-right (?) help control → Support). 2. Locate the Documentation section title after the page loads.
user-support-page-mrkjukp2-draft-4.expected: The "Documentation" title is present and visible on the Support page after load.

user-support-page-mrkjukp2-draft-5.steps: Open the Support page (left sidebar → bottom-right (?) help control → Support). 2. Verify the Documentation description text is presented to the user.
user-support-page-mrkjukp2-draft-5.expected: The user can see the text "Find answers to common questions and comprehensive guides" on the Support page.

login: Verify the primary Login page content is visible.
Verify the email, GitHub, and Google login methods are visible.
Verify the Sign up prompt is visible.
Verify Sign up navigation and the Log in prompt.
Verify invalid email formats are rejected.
Verify sensitive authentication data and internal errors are not exposed.
user-support-page-mrkjukp2: Find answers to common questions and comprehensive guides" text is visible.
3. Documentation button which will navigate to Documentation page is visible and clickable and navigates to that page on clicking.
Find answers to common questions and comprehensive guides" is visible to user

addedLinks.via: Navigate directly to Settings from the landing search page
